summary: Iteration 4 backend testing complete. 16 new tests added covering mood/journal mirroring, Claude AI distress auto-attach, manual AI regeneration endpoint, healing parks search, and per-user park bookmark toggle/isolation. All 16 iter4 tests + full regression of 68 backend tests pass (27 iter1 + 16 iter2 + 9 iter3 + 16 iter4 = 68/68 green). Updated iter1 mood test for new {entry, journal_entry} response shape and updated iter3 test #05 for newly-added 24h POST throttling on /api/insights/weekly.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1391 lines - well over the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into routers: routes/mood.py, routes/journal.py, routes/healing.py, routes/insights.py, routes/auth.py, plus a services/ layer for Claude/Whisper calls.

detect_distress() uses substring matching on lowercase content; this can produce false positives on common words containing the keywords (e.g., 'fail' inside 'failsafe'). Consider word-boundary regex matching with \b for better precision.

maybe_attach_ai_response swallows ALL exceptions silently (logger.exception then proceeds) - good for best-effort but the client has no signal that AI generation failed. Consider adding an optional 'ai_response_status' field ('pending'|'ok'|'failed') so the UI can show a retry affordance.

POST /api/mood with note now performs a synchronous Claude call (~5s) when distress is detected - blocks the mood check-in API. Consider firing the AI response asynchronously (background task) and letting the client poll/subscribe for the journal entry update, otherwise UX will feel laggy during crisis moments.

GET /api/healing/parks performs an extra DB roundtrip to fetch bookmarks even when no user is authenticated (it only skips if token is missing). Logic is fine, but consider using $lookup or caching bookmarked place_ids on the user document for hot path.

POST /api/journal/{entry_id}/ai-response will overwrite an existing ai_response without warning - intentional 'regenerate' semantics but undocumented. Add a note to the API or keep a history of past responses.

Manual ai-response endpoint always invokes Claude even on neutral entries (no distress check). This is by-design (manual override) but could be exploited to inflate Claude cost. Consider per-user rate limiting (e.g., 5/hour).

linked_journal_id is set on the mood doc via a second update_one after insertion - racey if a parallel list query reads between insert and update. Insert with linked_journal_id pre-populated would be cleaner.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (TestMood updated for new {entry, journal_entry} response shape)
/app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py (test_05 updated for new 24h POST throttling behavior)
test_credentials: Admin admin@echoaid.com / Admin@EchoAid2026 verified. Iter4 tests use ephemeral users TEST_i4A_*, TEST_i4B_* via register+verify+login.
seed_data_creation: TestHealingBookmarks creates ephemeral park_bookmarks rows on taman_suropati and taman_menteng; all bookmarks toggled off at end-of-test for cleanliness. TestJournal/Mood entries persist under ephemeral user accounts and are scoped to those users only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter4 changes: POST /api/mood now returns {entry, journal_entry} (journal_entry=null when no note). POST /api/journal and the mirrored journal entry auto-attach Claude Sonnet 4.5 ai_response when detect_distress() returns true (mood in {low,anxious} OR distress keyword in content). POST /api/journal/{id}/ai-response manually regenerates. GET /api/healing/parks supports ?q=... case-insensitive search across name/subtitle/tags; when authed, items include bookmarked:bool. POST /api/healing/parks/{place_id}/bookmark toggles; GET /api/healing/bookmarks lists user's. Cross-tenant isolation verified. ALSO: server now throttles POST /api/insights/weekly within 24h (returns cached doc with throttled:true) - this was implemented after iter3 review; test_iteration3.py::test_05 was updated to match. Claude calls take 3-6s; all timeouts set to 120s. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is working.
rca of the issue: N/A - all flows working as specified. Only test-side updates required: (1) iter1 backend_test.py TestMood asserted on old single-entry shape, updated to read body.entry/body.journal_entry; (2) iter3 test_05 expected new insight_id on each POST but server added 24h throttle returning cached doc, test updated to assert throttling instead.
